title: A Golden Opportunity for a Beleaguered WTO
content: By adopting the Investment Facilitation for Development Agreement, the World Trade Organization would brighten developing economies’ future prospects at a time of rising economic uncertainty. This would also send a powerful signal that, despite the challenges it faces, the WTO can still deliver tangible results.
creator: Karl P. Sauvant
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Far Right’s False Pacifism Is Endangering Europe
content: Europe's nationalist parties talk endlessly about defending European countries, but they are the least willing to confront hostile foreign governments. By taking a weak security posture, these leaders are practically inviting other countries to escalate their threats to Europe’s way of life.
creator: Nicu Popescu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Is America’s Goal in Cuba?
content: With the US fuel blockade creating a humanitarian crisis, Cuba’s current stalemate with the United States cannot last indefinitely. But it remains to be seen whether the Trump administration will focus solely on opening the market or insist that economic reforms are accompanied by political liberalization.
creator: Jorge G. Castañeda
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is Honesty the Best Policy for the International Order?
content: Instead of ensuring greater consistency in the application of global rules or inspiring reforms to existing institutions, Western leaders’ newfound frankness about the international order seems to be advancing quite different ends. If this is where honesty leads, we may soon miss the "fictional" world we have lost.
creator: Sophie Eisentraut
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Trump Gets Wrong About the Cultural Logic Driving Iran
content: US President Donald Trump’s Iran strategy rests on the premise that economic and military force will eventually compel the regime to back down. But such approaches tend to backfire in societies where preservation of honor and reputation dictates defiance.
creator: Michele Gelfand
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Solving the Tuberculosis Puzzle
content: A new class of rapid molecular tests for TB that can be deployed far closer to where people first seek care could significantly close the diagnostic gap and slow the spread of the world’s deadliest infectious disease. But harnessing the tests' transformative potential requires significant political will.
creator: Anafi Mataka
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Russia Is the Biggest Winner of the Iran War
content: While the reasons US President Donald Trump has given for the war against the Islamic Republic do not hold up, it is already clear that Russian President Vladimir Putin stands to gain the most from it. With oil prices surging, Russia is poised to reap an enormous windfall that will help sustain its own war against Ukraine.
creator: Chris Patten
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Iran Is Sanctioning America
content: By choking off the Strait of Hormuz to oil flowing from pro-US Persian Gulf countries, while still shipping a substantial amount of its own oil to China, Iran has effectively slapped painful sanctions on the US. If these de facto sanctions last, they will impose high economic costs on many Americans – and political costs on Donald Trump.
creator: Simon Johnson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Africa Is Forging Its Own Green Future
content: Despite bearing the brunt of a climate crisis it did not create, Africa is being held back by a financial system that has long failed to meet its needs. But instead of waiting for outside solutions, African institutions are laying the groundwork for a green transition that advances both development and climate resilience.
creator: Elias Kagumya
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is the Private Credit Boom Going Bust?
content: Since 2008, private credit has expanded into a $3.5 trillion industry, driven by private equity and operating outside the reach of traditional banking rules. As cracks begin to appear, the risks embedded in a system built on opaque valuations and rising leverage are becoming harder to ignore.
creator: Brian Judge
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Telling Americans the Truth About Immigration
content: Donald Trump’s mass deportations are raising the prices of critical goods and services, gutting a key driver of innovation, and eliminating just about the only force working to rein in the national debt. With immigrants less likely to commit crimes than native-born Americans, to call his crackdown misguided is an understatement.
creator: Jeffrey Frankel
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Selective Outrage Won’t End the Iran War
content: The United Nations Security Council’s resolution condemning Iran’s retaliatory strikes on US bases and energy facilities in neighboring countries ignores why they occurred. The resolution is less about international law than about protecting oil flows, global economic stability, and the Gulf states’ privileged status.
creator: Jayati Ghosh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump Is Burying His Own Security Strategy
content: US President Donald Trump has leapt feet first into a major new Middle East war, the objectives of which are changing by the day. As the conflict escalates, the endgame scenarios are growing increasingly bleak and complicated, making a mockery of the spirit and letter of Trump's three-month-old National Security Strategy.
creator: Carl Bildt
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What the Iran Crisis Means for Middle Powers
content: For solidarity among the world's middle powers to matter, it must be used to create a new system of states that are governed by the rule of law and committed to democratic norms and principles. The alternative is a world governed by power alone, where dependence is weaponized, and public consent becomes an inconvenience.
creator: Ekrem İmamoğlu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Will AGI Really Be the “Last Invention”?
content: Listen to leading voices in Silicon Valley, and you might come to believe that “solving intelligence” is sufficient to “solve everything else.” But as seductive as this claim about AI’s potential may be, it rests on a number of assumptions that do not withstand scrutiny.
creator: Carl Benedikt Frey
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Building the Energy Resilience ASEAN+3 Needs
content: Climate shocks, rapid technological change, and geopolitical turmoil are adding to the pressure on energy systems across Southeast Asia, underscoring the importance of enhancing resilience. Strengthening energy resilience is not only an energy-policy priority; it is a macroeconomic imperative.
creator: Yasuto Watanabe
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Habermas and the World We Have Lost
content: With his death at 96, Jürgen Habermas, a titan of postwar political philosophy leaves behind a world that seems to be dismantling everything he defended, both as a scholar and as an engaged public intellectual. He and the approach he championed will be missed.
creator: Peter J. Verovšek
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Africa Isn’t the World’s “Climate Solution”
content: Africa is increasingly portrayed as a global leader on climate solutions. But this narrative presents climate justice as a technical financing challenge, rather than a matter of holding historic emitters accountable, thereby reinforcing structural arrangements that sustain the continent’s continued exploitation.
creator: Martha Bekele
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: America’s Permanent Tariff Uncertainty Will Drive Up Costs
content: The US Supreme Court’s rejection of the Trump administration’s legal justification for tariffs, and the administration's quest for a new one, has created a patchwork of temporary measures, sectoral probes, bilateral bargaining, and national-security exceptions. There will now be three additional costs associated with US trade policy.
creator: Pinelopi Koujianou Goldberg
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: This Energy Shock Demands a Green Industrial Strategy
content: In an era of geopolitical tumult, economic resilience requires changing not only the kinds of energy we consume, but also how, where, and by whom things are produced. Fortunately, through mission-oriented green industrial strategies, governments can help secure living standards and build economic resilience simultaneously.
creator: Mariana Mazzucato
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: US Institutional Decay Is Threatening Global Finance
content: Even through tumultuous political cycles, the Federal Reserve, the Securities and Exchange Commission, and the Federal Trade Commission have been able to signal that US markets operate under clear, impersonal, reliably enforced rules. But this is no longer true, and the implications for the rest of the world are dire.
creator: Jayant Sinha
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A New Opening for WTO Reform
content: World Trade Organization members agree that structural reform is necessary, but they diverge on what that should look like. A good first step would be for the trade ministers attending the WTO’s 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon, to recommit the trade system to an overarching focus on sustainable development.
creator: Daniel Esty
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Gulf Is Becoming Uninsurable
content: The Gulf states never anticipated Iran’s strategy of pressuring the US by targeting them, because they never anticipated US indifference to their security. Their reputation for safety and stability in tatters, they will pay a heavy price.
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Real Fallout From Trump’s Tariffs
content: Donald Trump might have hoped that his “Liberation Day” tariffs would punish China and boost the competitiveness of American industries, but they have instead had the opposite effect. Worse, the policy has had far-reaching unintended consequences that few policymakers and commentators have yet to reckon with.
creator: Jun Du
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page
